joke.raw_joke: My girlfriend told me she wanted to see a diamond on our anniversary. — I took her to a baseball game.

script:
- setup: "My girlfriend told me she wanted to see a diamond on our anniversary."
- punchline: "I took her to a baseball game."
- tag: ~

changes_made: Minimal — raw was already correct. The setup plants the expectation cleanly (girlfriend + anniversary + diamond = ring), the punchline subverts it with the only other kind of diamond that matters. No words to cut, no structure to adjust. The joke is already at minimum viable size.

caption_notes: Two cards. Setup: "My girlfriend told me she wanted to see a diamond on our anniversary." Punchline: "I took her to a baseball game." — clean sentence, period only. No emphasis needed. The audience does the work of connecting the two meanings.

timing_notes: Setup delivered at steady conversational pace — a man relaying what his girlfriend said. The pause before the punchline should feel like genuine consideration on his part, as if he solved a problem. 0.4s pause. Punchline flat and matter-of-fact — he took her to a game, the request was fulfilled. He is not apologizing for this. He never considered there was anything to apologize for.

quality_flag: strong
